admission_type: open access with mandatory admission test (free access, but requires passing a verification of personal readiness)

academic_requirements.summary: Free-access (non-restricted) master's programme that nonetheless requires meeting curricular credit requirements and passing a verification of the graduate's personal readiness unless exempt. Admission requires a bachelor's degree (or equivalent) of at least 180 CFU/ECTS, minimum credits in specified industrial-engineering scientific sectors, and English at B2 level.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A first-cycle degree (Laurea / Bachelor's degree) or an equivalent qualification obtained abroad, with at least 180 CFU/ECTS.
academic_requirements.curricular_credit_requirements: At least 36 CFU in the scientific sectors related to the L-9 Industrial Engineering class, and at least 45 CFU in the scientific sectors related to the L-9 Industrial Engineering class. Qualifications outside industrial engineering are individually assessed by the University and the programme council.
academic_requirements.minimum_grade_or_gpa: No minimum grade is required to apply, but a final bachelor's grade of at least 99/110 (or 9/10 of the maximum) or an ECTS classification of 'A' or 'B' exempts the candidate from the admission test.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Non-EU candidates residing abroad with a foreign qualification apply through UniGeApply for pre-evaluation before enrolment; a maximum number of non-EU places is set each year (50 for A.Y. 2025/26).

language_requirements.required_level: English at least equivalent to CEFR B2.
accepted_proofs: A valid B2 (or higher) English certificate obtained no more than 3 years ago, among those recognised by the University (CLAT list, https://clat.unige.it/en/CertificazioniRiconosciute).
Passing the free B2 English test organised periodically by the University Language Centre (CLAT).
A bachelor's or master's degree taught entirely in English, with supporting official university documentation.
exemption_rules: Holding a degree delivered entirely in English satisfies the language requirement without a separate certificate.
In the absence of a certificate, English proficiency is verified by the University Language Centre / during the personal-readiness verification.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Verified at the admission/pre-evaluation stage; candidates without an accepted certificate are assessed by the University Language Centre or during the readiness verification.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Verification of Adequacy of the Graduate's Personal Readiness (admission test). NOT a TOLC.
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: All applicants who do not qualify for an automatic exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.format: A written test (possibly in telematic/remote mode) before a committee, covering Fluid Dynamics, Applied Thermodynamics and Heat Transfer, Energy Systems and Machines, Electrical Systems and Chemical Processes.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: [uncertain] For A.Y. 2025/26 the published sessions were 15 Sep 2025, 17 Oct 2025, 14 Nov 2025, 16 Jan 2026, 24 Feb 2026 and 4 Mar 2026. Equivalent A.Y. 2026/27 dates were not yet published on the page at the time of research.
entry_exam_or_test.exemption: Candidates with a bachelor's/master's degree from an Italian institution with a final grade of at least 99/110 (9/10 of the maximum) or an ECTS 'A'/'B' classification are exempt.

rounds: Spring session for EU citizens and non-EU residents in Italy (UniGeApply): opens 9 April 2026; master's degrees close 28 August 2026.
Enrolment and fee payment follow the UniGe enrolment calendar after the curricular check and (where applicable) the admission test.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: [uncertain] Programme-specific first-year enrolment dates for A.Y. 2026/27 follow the central UniGe enrolment calendar; a programme-specific date was not separately published.
application_deadline_eu.notes: This is a free-access programme; EU/resident applicants are not ranked, but they must satisfy the curricular requirements and pass the admission test unless exempt.

rounds: Fall session for non-EU candidates requiring a visa (UniGeApply): opens 26 November 2025; institution-wide close 20 March 2026; pre-evaluation results by 22 May 2026.
Energy Engineering is listed among the English-taught master's degrees in the UniGeApply non-EU pre-evaluation selection.
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: After receiving eligibility via UniGeApply, visa applicants complete the Universitaly pre-enrolment and then apply for the study visa.
application_deadline_non_eu.early_closure: None published. Energy Engineering is not among the three programmes with a known fall-session early closure (Medical-Pharmaceutical Biotechnology, Maritime Science and Technology, Computer Engineering bachelor).
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU visa applicants must use the fall session; the A.Y. 2026/27 fall window closed 20 March 2026.

required_documents.notes: Uploaded on UniGeApply for non-EU pre-evaluation.
Evidence of the first-cycle qualification (180 CFU minimum).

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of records (list of exams with credits and grades)
required_documents.notes: Used to verify the curricular credit requirements (36 + 45 CFU in L-9 sectors).

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency certificate (B2) or proof of English-medium instruction
required_documents.notes: B2 certificate no older than 3 years, a degree taught entirely in English, or verification by the University Language Centre.
Required for non-EU applicants on UniGeApply.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study-visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Completed after UniGeApply eligibility, before applying for the study visa.

source_quotes.quote: Class: LM-30 R ENERGY AND NUCLEAR ENGINEERING
all modules are taught in English, free English courses are offered to En2 students and about 60% of En2 students come from abroad
This degree course is free access, but requires passing a test.
At least 36 CFU ... in the scientific sectors related to the L-9 Industrial Engineering Class; At least 45 CFU ... in the scientific sectors related to the L-9 Industrial Engineering Class
Knowledge of the English language is required, at least equivalent to the European (CEFR) B2 level.

uncertainty_notes: The catalogue tagged this course as English+Italian, but the official programme page states all modules are taught in English (with free English courses for students); teaching_language set to English accordingly.
Only A.Y. 2025/26 admission-test session dates were published on the requisites page at research time; 2026/27 session dates were not yet listed.
Programme-specific first-year EU enrolment dates were not separately published; the institution-wide UniGeApply spring window and the central enrolment calendar apply.
No programme-specific non-EU early closure is published; Energy Engineering follows the institution-wide fall close of 20 March 2026.
